verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein (ectodomain + TM domain + short cytoplasmic tail) expressed exclusively in testis/sperm. Its ectodomain reaches the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, but only: (1) in sperm — a narrowly restricted germline/gamete lineage (tissue_restricted_surface), and (2) after the acrosome reaction — an activation-triggered exocytic event that translocates IZUMO1 from the inner acrosomal membrane to the equatorial plasma membrane surface. In acrosome-intact sperm it is not accessible from the extracellular face. Both the cell-type restriction and the activation-state gating prevent a flat 'yes' verdict.
